intro: Hepetis ochroleuca, commonly known as False Rue-anthemum or Leopard's Bane, is a flowering plant species within the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, exhibiting potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can aid in the treatment of various ailments including fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Hepetis ochroleuca include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory qualities.
